column4: BTC rallied from ~$998 to ~$19k through 2017 but this run is excluded from the five named consensus regimes; labelling ranging to remain conservative ahead of the Dec-17 ATH.
BTC fell from the Dec-17-2017 ATH (~$19,783) to the Dec-15-2018 cycle low ($3,122, -84%); aligned with the ICO-bubble unwind, Mt Gox trustee selling, and global regulatory crackdown on token sales.
Post-capitulation chop: BTC recovered to a June-26-2019 local peak near $13.8k (Facebook Libra hype) then drifted back to ~$7k by end-2019; no unambiguous trend so labelled ranging.
BTC slid from ~$10.5k (mid-Feb local high) to an intraday low of ~$3,858 on Mar-12-13 2020 ("Black Thursday") as the WHO declared COVID-19 a pandemic (Mar-11) and global risk assets liquidated.
BTC rallied from the COVID trough (~$3.9k) to the Nov-10-2021 ATH of $69,044; powered by Fed QE, the May-2020 halving, institutional adoption (MicroStrategy, Tesla), and DeFi/NFT speculative cycle.

BTC fell from the $69k ATH to the Nov-21-2022 cycle low of ~$15,460 (-78%); compounded by Fed hiking cycle, Terra/LUNA collapse (May 2022), Celsius/3AC failures (June 2022) and FTX collapse (Nov-11-2022).

Post-FTX recovery and consolidation; BTC traded $16k-$44k through 2023 (Mar-2023 banking-crisis rally, BlackRock ETF filing June-15-2023, Grayscale court win Aug-29-2023) — directional but excluded from named bull regimes.
BTC rallied from ~$46k on the Jan-10-2024 SEC spot-ETF approval to the Oct-6-2025 ATH of ~$126,198; driven by record spot-ETF inflows, the Apr-2024 halving, and post-election US policy tailwinds.

Post-ATH correction following the Oct-6-2025 peak; BTC sold off toward ~$100k in November and into the $80-90k range through December — not one of the five named consensus regimes, so labelled ranging.
